name: Higgs vacuum (meta)stability
summary: Renormalisation-group running of the Higgs quartic coupling lambda(mu) under SM dynamics drives lambda below zero around mu ~ 10^10 GeV (driven primarily by the top Yukawa). This means the SM vacuum is metastable on a timescale much longer than the age of the universe but not absolutely stable. The Higgs and top masses sit remarkably close to the boundary between stability and instability — a striking near-criticality coincidence.

characteristic_numbers.note: For m_H = 125 GeV and m_t = 173 GeV, NNLO running puts the zero-crossing of lambda near the GUT scale, with stability excluded at ~98% CL (Degrassi et al. 2012, arXiv:1205.6497).

characteristic_numbers.expected: any positive time would suffice anthropically
characteristic_numbers.note: Vastly longer than the cosmological age, so the metastability is not in tension with our existence. The puzzle is the near-coincidence with the stability boundary, not the tunneling rate itself.

principles_stressed.note: The closeness of lambda to zero at high scale resembles other naturalness puzzles where SM parameters take 'critical' values. Suggests either (a) high-scale new physics aligning lambda(M_Pl) ~ 0, or (b) anthropic / multiverse selection.

history: Cabibbo, Maiani, Parisi, Petronzio (1979) raised stability concern. Quantitative NNLO analysis by Degrassi-Di Vita-Elias-Miro-Espinosa-Giudice-Isidori-Strumia (2012, arXiv:1205.6497). Bezrukov-Magnin-Shaposhnikov (2008) tied to Higgs inflation. Active since the Higgs discovery in 2012 fixed lambda at low energy.

references.citation: Degrassi, Di Vita, Elias-Miro, Espinosa, Giudice, Isidori, Strumia, 'Higgs mass and vacuum stability in the Standard Model at NNLO', JHEP 08, 098 (2012)
